channel.title: Piero Savastano
channel.description: Started as a young research scientist specialized on computational neuroscience, cognitive psychology, artificial life and artificial intelligence. Ended up creating videos, maintaining an AI open source framework and consulting internationally on AI.

item.title: The Vibe Coder's Impostor Syndrome
item.description: Feeling inadequate in front of AI isn't a sign you don't belong - it's the sign you're actually putting yourself to the test.

item.title: Vibecodo e Vibegodo
item.description: A short, half-joking rant about open source, open weights, and the one freedom I refuse to give up.

item.title: How to Squeeze Money Out of a Vibecoder
item.description: The bottleneck has moved from writing code to securing it - and that shift is a business opportunity.

item.title: Already Out of Tokens?
item.description: Why usage of non-American models is exploding, what you actually save, and the catch nobody mentions.

item.title: AI Agents: Writing a Skill System From Scratch (Vibe-Coding the Cheshire Cat)
item.description: A hands-on vibe-coding session building an agent skill system from zero with the Cheshire Cat framework.

item.title: The Truth About Fable, After the Ban
item.description: The Mythos-to-Fable hype cycle, the ban, and the compute economics that quietly make these models dumber.

item.title: The Richard Dawkins Controversy
item.description: Dawkins says he thinks Claude is conscious. Everyone called him a senile old man. I think he nailed it.

item.title: Reading Brautigan's 'All Watched Over by Machines of Loving Grace'
item.description: Where the title of Amodei’s famous AI essay comes from - a 1967 Richard Brautigan poem, read aloud.

item.title: You Need a Passport to Use AI
item.description: ID and age checks are coming to AI, and OpenAI is turning into a kind of token bank you have to register with.

item.title: Which AI Do I Buy for My Company or Department?
item.description: AI is not a single product to buy in one block - it is separable pieces, and knowing them keeps you out of a vendor cage.

item.title: Cheshire Cat 2 Supports Skills
item.description: A 20-minute experiment giving the Cheshire Cat 2 framework agent skills - and the open questions about MCP and safety.

item.title: What's New in MCP
item.description: A new version of the Model Context Protocol lands July 28th: stateless transport, deprecations, tasks and MCP apps.

item.title: Work Shifts to the Bottlenecks
item.description: AI does not just automate tasks - it moves human work to whatever constraint is now scarce.

item.title: Is It Worth Writing Code?
item.description: A live conversation with Giuseppe Funicello on whether writing code, learning frameworks, and building products still pays off.

item.title: The Truth About the Fable Ban
item.description: Why the US government restricted Anthropic Fable and Mythos models, and why I think surveillance is the real story.

item.title: Live on RAI Radio1
item.description: I'm on Italian public radio talking Anthropic, the SpaceX valuation, and what AI means for programmers.

item.title: The Truth About Mythos and Fable 5
item.description: Why premium AI models are sold "by the sip" - and why the "too dangerous" safety framing is really about B2B pricing.

item.title: The 3 Loops of AI: Token, Tool, and Task
item.description: The word "loop" gets thrown around a lot in AI - but there are at least three nested ones, from single tokens up to whole tasks.

item.title: Fable 5 and Agents in a Loop
item.description: Fable 5 finally ships, and top agent builders now write loops instead of prompts - at a steep token cost.

item.title: MCP vs Skills, and Women in Tech
item.description: Which to cover next - MCP or agent skills - plus a hard, honest look at bringing more women into AI.

item.title: Anthropic: World Champions of Eloquent Gibberish
item.description: Anthropic keeps calling for an AI slowdown - but the simpler explanation is they fell behind on compute.

item.title: Mind and Brain Are the Same Thing
item.description: A neuroscientist pushes back on mind-body dualism: intelligence is physical, machines understand, and we are beautiful biological machines.

item.title: Help Pope Leo Smash the Data Centers
item.description: The new encyclical on AI is sharper and more political than I expected - and it's a little embarrassing that the Pope has to be the one saying it.

item.title: Vibe-Coding Session on the Pope's Encyclical: Highlights
item.description: Reading the new AI encyclical, building a Pope-vs-Big-Tech video game with Claude Code, and finding the exact limits of vibe coding.

item.title: Faggin Hasn't Understood AI
item.description: Inventing the microchip doesn't make you right forever. You don't need quantum mechanics to explain intelligence - it's a physical process.

item.title: Two Branches of AI, What Will Happen?
item.description: You have a million euros to bet on the future of AI: the personal digital twin that lives on your computer, or intelligence as a networked service. Where do you put it?

item.title: AI as Coordination, Not Just Automation
item.description: We're used to framing AI as automation. But its deeper role might be coordination - aligning people and organizations where before we had to do it by hand.

item.title: Low-Level Programming? Stuff for Old People
item.description: Every generation has its grumpy old-timers. The pointer-and-memory diehards were ours. The next one? Us - the ones who still insist on writing code at all.

item.title: You Still Haven't Understood What MCP Is For
item.description: 'MCP is dead, now there are CLIs and skills.' No. MCP was never about the toys on your laptop - it's about composable AI agents used by groups of people over the network.

item.title: Why the Vibe-Coded Project Is Worth ZERO
item.description: My problem is not open source or AI: it is that software churned out in two hours with zero idea behind it has no scarcity, no effort, and therefore no value.

item.title: How to Sell AI Agents: The Main Obstacle
item.description: The hardest part of selling AI is getting people who expect exactness from a computer to accept that a statistical model is uncertain by definition - and to design around that.

item.title: Why SaaS Died with AI
item.description: AI is raw intelligence sold by consumption, not by subscription - and that quietly breaks the economics that made software-as-a-service work.

item.title: Happy Liberation (from American AI)
item.description: On Liberation Day, a case against central authorities deciding what counts as truth - and for slowly freeing ourselves from the foreign providers that shape our language and our machines.

item.title: Molotov on Altman's House - the AI Anger Phase
item.description: Reading the AI transition through the psychology of grief: after a long denial phase, I now see the anger phase arriving, and it worries me.

item.title: The Truth About Anthropic and Mythos
item.description: When AI CEOs make public statements, read the subtext: negate what they say, ask what interest they have, and check the historical context - it leads you straight to the truth.

item.title: Old Age Teaches: From GPT-2 to Mythos
item.description: The "too dangerous to release" routine is not new: OpenAI already played that card with GPT-2, and it is a magic trick to lure the gullible, not a safety measure.

item.title: Reply to @Devmy - Which AI to Use in Your Company
item.description: Stop agonizing over which AI vendor to lock into. Treat intelligence like electricity from a pipe, start anywhere, and focus on standards and practices so you can switch whenever you want.

item.title: Agentic Engineering - 6 Tips to Use Fewer Tokens
item.description: Six practical habits to spend fewer tokens and keep your coding agent sharp: clean context, package skills, and ask precise questions.

item.title: Skills vs MCP for AI Agents
item.description: Two ways to make your agents composable: MCP servers for networked services shared by groups, and skills for local, reusable workflows.

item.title: From GPT-3 (2020) to Claude Code (2026) - Easy Whiteboard Explanation
item.description: A whiteboard walk through the three big jumps that took us from a language model to an agent: GPT-3, ChatGPT, and Claude Code.

item.title: AI Destroys the Job Market (Translators vs Programmers)
item.description: What automation already did to translators is the same storm now hitting developers and every other white-collar job.

item.title: Claude Opus Is Smarter Than Me
item.description: Refusing to admit that these models can out-code you given the right context is just denial, and I make a living teaching people to get out of that loop.

item.title: How to Get Started with Agentic Development
item.description: Four pieces of advice to get past your resistance and start experimenting with agentic development - because none of this is black and white.

item.title: They Insult Me Because I Use AI
item.description: People call my AI communication toxic and say I am selling FOMO. The truth is simpler: big change lands like trauma, and a lot of people are stuck in denial.

item.title: If You Don't Try AI Agents You're Incompetent
item.description: We are already on the second generation of AI. The first was chatbots that only spit out text; the second is agents that actually act on the world, and if you are still arguing about chatbots you have missed the jump.

item.title: I've Already Changed Jobs 4 Times Because of AI: My Story
item.description: From neural-network researcher to web developer to data scientist to open-source maintainer: living right next to AI has forced me to reinvent my career at least four times, and it is far from over.

item.title: Information
item.description: A one-minute take on Shannon: information is the negative logarithm of probability, and on top of it we build understanding and intelligence.

item.title: Epstein vs ICE in Western Social Media Algorithms
item.description: Why do the Epstein files flood Western TikTok while ICE content quietly disappears? A short note on the probabilistic censorship built into recommendation engines.

item.title: Winner Takes All with Compute and Data Centers
item.description: This wave of AI automates cognition across the board, and whoever owns the infrastructure, energy and data takes it all - while the rest of us are left scrambling.

item.title: #vibechallenge
item.description: A three-way experiment to find out for yourself whether coding with AI is real or just hype: take a side project you never shipped and build it three different ways.

item.title: The TRUTH About Moltbook
item.description: AI agents did not build their own social network to organize a revolution. Behind the Moltbook hype are humans installing skills, editing prompts, and cashing in on the virality.

item.title: 4 Tips to Survive VIBE CODING
item.description: The software market is about to explode in size, coding has splintered into many nuanced styles, and if you are still resisting, it is emotion talking, not reason.

item.title: AI Is Way Smarter Than Me
item.description: Admitting that AI agents are smarter than me at writing code does not make me feel smaller. It just moves the question to what actually makes us human.

item.title: Software as a Commodity and Enshittification
item.description: What happened to photography when smartphones arrived may be happening to software now, and I want to think through where that leads for the attention economy.

item.title: Vibe Coding vs Spec-Driven Development
item.description: Vibe coding was a godsend that opened programming to everyone, and now spec-driven development is opening the professional, agent-driven side of the craft.

item.title: MCP: Let Me Train You
item.description: For a year I have been telling you to play with MCP, and now the signal is impossible to ignore: the whole Google suite is shipping MCP versions, and I am putting together training on it.

item.title: AI Agents Are Simply Software
item.description: The language model is stateless: it takes context in and spits one token out. An agent is just the software you wrap around it to give it memory and actions. Everything else follows from that.

item.title: Advice for Overcoming Impostor Syndrome in AI
item.description: I am behind, I am not capable, I feel useless: the three things my fellow technicians keep saying, and why that very uncertainty is the real sign of competence.

item.title: Amazon's BUY FOR ME and the Implications for E-commerce
item.description: With Buy for Me, Amazon sends an AI agent to buy products from stores outside its own platform on your behalf, and that is a much bigger shift than another nerdy automation demo.

item.title: ASMR on Artificial Intelligence, Episode 34
item.description: You do not need a grand plan to get into AI. Just start playing with it, and the ideas for your own work will come on their own.

item.title: ChatGPT's Apps Are MCP Servers - Reaction to @simone_rizzo98
item.description: Under the hood, ChatGPT apps are just MCP servers, and this is why MCP is quietly winning while Google A2A struggles.

item.title: You're a Manager of AI Agents
item.description: Vibe coding is giving way to structured, agent-driven development where you review pull requests instead of crossing your fingers.

item.title: MCP and A2A under the Linux Foundation
item.description: Both MCP and A2A now live under the Linux Foundation, and here is why the AI field badly needs these protocols to finally unify.

item.title: Dynamic Agents in Cheshire Cat v2
item.description: Most Python agent libraries treat the prompt, the model, the tools and the memory as static - and in Cheshire Cat v2 I think every one of those parts should be dynamic.

item.title: The Truth About the TOON Format
item.description: The new TOON format promises to save you tokens at scale, but you spend more tokens explaining it to the model than you ever save - and no, you are not scaling anyway.

item.title: Let's Read Anthropic's Research Together - INTROSPECTION in LLMs
item.description: A guided read-through of Anthropic's 'Signs of Introspection' paper: how concept vectors get injected into a network, and why the model can sometimes name what was done to it.

item.title: The Black Mirror of AI
item.description: AI is a black mirror: you can not see inside it, but it reflects what you think of yourself and of what makes humans special.

item.title: 3 Real-World Use Cases for AI: Doctor Visits, Job Interviews, and Civil Litigation
item.description: We keep talking about AI revolutionizing healthcare, hiring, and the courts, but the prerequisite for AI is data that exists and talks to itself, and in the real world that data simply is not there.

item.title: 4 Ways to Make Money with AI
item.description: From the con-artist grift to AI as a feature, AI first products, and platform plays - the four real ways people are making money with artificial intelligence.

item.title: Cheshire Cat AI - Version 2 PREVIEW
item.description: A first look at Cheshire Cat version 2: multi-chat, multi-agent, context-driven, MCP-connected, and still API first, built entirely around plugins.

item.title: What Is the Difference Between Tool and Resource in MCP?
item.description: A tool is a function the language model chooses to call; a resource is something read into context. Here is how the three MCP constructs actually differ.

item.title: How to Get Started with AI? Is It Worth It?
item.description: It is worth it precisely because everything is still fragmented and uncertain, and here is the concrete path in: mess with the online services, then learn context, memory and tools.

item.title: AI + Copy/Paste, Reaction to @MgpF
item.description: A journalist copy-pasting straight from ChatGPT is not a gaffe, it is the use case handed to you on a plate: the agent should send the work where it belongs, with human review by design.

item.title: AI Bubble Coming Soon
item.description: There are real signs of an AI bubble, but if it bursts it burns the greedy investors, not the technology - just like the dotcom crash never cancelled the web.

item.title: Wanna Marchi and Artificial Intelligence
item.description: AI is neither total automation nor useless hype. It lives on a continuous line between manual and automatic, and the honest work is figuring out where on that line your case belongs.

item.title: What Is an AI AGENT
item.description: A plain-language explainer: an agent is software that wraps a language model, building the prompt with retrieved context and tools and interpreting the response before it reaches you.

item.title: August 7, 2025
item.description: My hot take on the GPT-5 launch: incremental additions, a cringe-worthy live demo, and the clearest sign yet that OpenAI has stopped innovating.

item.title: AI 2027 Is Bullshit
item.description: The doomer scenario where AI takes over in two years misses the point: the short-term story is about infrastructure, power concentration, and how jobs change.

item.title: How to Make Estimates with AI
item.description: Why I keep refusing to build fully automatic quoting systems: language models do not do math, and the real business rules live as implicit knowledge in human heads.

item.title: The Bullshit of Desperate CEOs
item.description: When a CEO brags about firing his staff thanks to AI, it's usually a valuation stunt - and the real winners are the ones who own the data centers.

item.title: How Kenyans and Venezuelans Are Being Exploited by the AI Market
item.description: The reason ChatGPT is polite and safe is an invisible workforce of underpaid annotators in the poorest corners of the world, hunted for being educated and desperate.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Is Superior to Human Intelligence (No Contest)
item.description: Machines are already smarter than us in many ways, and pretending otherwise is a distraction from the real problem: who owns the infrastructure.

item.title: Transformer and Attention in LLMs
item.description: What actually sits inside the black box of a language model: the Transformer architecture, attention blocks, and why language is just a time series to a machine.

item.title: What Is RAG?
item.description: RAG sounds complicated, but the acronym already tells you everything: you retrieve relevant content and stuff it into the prompt so the model has the answer in front of it.

item.title: 'Computer Use' Agents vs 'As a Service' Agents
item.description: Two kinds of AI agents are taking shape - the ones that grab your mouse and click around for you, and the ones that quietly talk to network services and just get it done.

item.title: ChatGPT Is Like a COCKROACH
item.description: After a nighttime fight with a giant cockroach, a reflection on collective intelligence, embodiment, and why AI might be less a mind in a box and more a swarm-like form of artificial life.

item.title: Zero Click Search - AI Dominates Web Search
item.description: AI engines are answering search queries directly, and fewer people are clicking through to the sites that produced the information. This changes everything about how we make and monetize content.

item.title: Down with the Sales Guy
item.description: Technicians feel they know nothing about AI while bullshitters sell it and hand the real work to us. Stop it: you already know AI, raise your prices, and refuse to be the hidden fixer.

item.title: Guide to AI Basics
item.description: A plain-language tour of the terms you keep hearing: model, agent, RAG, MCP, GPUs and data centers, and the Cheshire Cat.

item.title: UX + AI Principles
item.description: An appeal to designers and technicians: flat information architecture, mixed conversational and graphic interfaces, and human-in-the-loop as the design frontier of AI products.

item.title: AI News - World Models, Software 3.0, A2A
item.description: Three things worth noting: Meta's video world models, Karpathy's Software 3.0 talk, and Google donating the A2A protocol to the Linux Foundation.

item.title: Corporate Propaganda via AI R&D
item.description: When Big Tech publishes AI research, read past the method and the results - there is almost always a corporate message baked into the conclusions.

item.title: Hello World
item.description: Inaugurating this new website
